concept: Hemp / Strong Plant Fiber / Durable Textile
description: A tall plant with strong fibers used for rope and cloth; the workhorse fiber. 'Henoa' names what holds—the plant that gives strength without breaking.
sound_symbolism: The word holds hardy. 'He' breathes with living vitality, plant growing. 'No' knots with binding strength, fiber tough. Then 'a' opens to versatile use.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo henoa' (the hemp). Strongest plant fiber. For rope, canvas, durable cloth.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Sustainable strength—fast-growing, needs little water, replaces petroleum products.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Hemp built ships—sails and ropes of the age of exploration.
